title: Prime Factorization Algorithm
description: Decompose a number into its prime factors
mermaid: graph TD A1[Start|Triggers & Inputs] -- Input Number (N) --> B1; style A1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#FFFFFF B1[Initialize Factor (F) = 2|Structures & Objects]; style B1 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000000 B1 --> C1; C1[Initialize Prime Factors List (PF) = []|Structures & Objects]; style C1 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000000 C1 --> D1; D1[Is F * F <= N?|Processing & Operations]; style D1 fill:#51cf66,color:#FFFFFF D1 -- Yes --> E1; D1 -- No --> K1; E1[Is N divisible by F?|Processing & Operations]; style E1 fill:#51cf66,color:#FFFFFF E1 -- Yes --> F1; E1 -- No --> J1; F1[Append F to PF|Processing & Operations]; style F1 fill:#51cf66,color:#FFFFFF F1 --> G1; G1[Divide N by F|Processing & Operations]; style G1 fill:#51cf66,color:#FFFFFF G1 --> H1; H1[Update N = N / F|Intermediates & States]; style H1 fill:#74c0fc,color:#FFFFFF H1 --> E1; J1[Increment F = F + 1|Processing & Operations]; style J1 fill:#51cf66,color:#FFFFFF J1 --> B2; B2[Update F = F|Intermediates & States]; style B2 fill:#74c0fc,color:#FFFFFF B2 --> D1; K1[Is N > 1?|Processing & Operations]; style K1 fill:#51cf66,color:#FFFFFF K1 -- Yes --> L1; K1 -- No --> M1; L1[Append N to PF|Processing & Operations]; style L1 fill:#51cf66,color:#FFFFFF L1 --> M1; M1[Prime Factors List (PF)|Products & Outputs]; style M1 fill:#b197fc,color:#FFFFFF M1 --> N1; N1[End|Products & Outputs]; style N1 fill:#b197fc,color:#FFFFFF
entities: N) --> B1; style A1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#FFFFFF B1[Initialize Factor (F) = 2|Structures & Objects
